column1: 2 ^ In Dominō confīdō~; ¿~quōmodō dīcitis animae meae
column2: «~Transmigrā in montem sīcut passer~»~? ^ In the Lord I put my trust~; how then do you say to my soul
column3: "Flee to the mountain like a sparrow"?

column1: 3 ^ Quoniam ecce peccātōrēs intendērunt arcum~; parāvērunt sagittās suās in faretrā
column2: ut sagittent in obscūrō rēctōs corde. ^ For behold the wicked have bent their bow~; they have prepared their arrows in the quiver
column3: to shoot in the dark the upright of heart~;
quid fēcit~? ^ For the things which thou hast perfected
column4: they destroyed~; but what has the just man done?

column1: 5 ^ Dominus in templō sanctō suō~; Dominus in caelō sēdis ejus. Oculī ejus in pauperem respiciunt~; palpebrae ejus interrogant fīliōs hominum. ^ The Lord is in his holy temple
column2: the Lord's throne is in heaven. His eyes look on the poor man~; his eyelids examine the sons of men.

column1: 6 ^ Dominus interrogat jūstum et impium~; quī autem dīligit inīquitātem
column2: ōdit animam suam. ^ The Lord trieth the just and the wicked~; but he that loveth iniquity

column1: 7 ^ Pluet super peccātōrēs laqueōs~; ignis et sulfur
column3: pars calicis eōrum. ^ He shall rain snares upon sinners~; fire and brimstone
column5: shall be the portion of their cup.

column2: et jūstitiās dīlēxit~; aequitātem vīdit vultus ejus. ^ For the Lord is just
column3: and hath loved justice~; his countenance hath beheld righteousness.
